text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Georgie. Neil, we're talking about protein today.
zh: 我是Neil。我是Georgie。Neil,我们今天聊聊蛋白质。

text: What type of protein do you usually eat? Well, I like all kinds of proteins – fish, bit of chicken.
zh: 你平时都吃什么类型的蛋白质呢?嗯,各种蛋白质我都喜欢——鱼啊,还有一点鸡肉。

text: But I think I really like an egg. An egg egg is my favourite kind of protein.
zh: 不过我觉得我真的很喜欢鸡蛋。鸡蛋是我最喜欢的蛋白质来源。

text: I was going to say that too. I'm a big fan of breakfast foods and eggs are my favourite. Well, you can't beat a good breakfast.
zh: 我也正想这么说呢。我特别喜欢早餐食物,鸡蛋是我的最爱。是啊,没什么能比得上一顿丰盛的早餐。

text: Of all the main food groups, such as carbohydrate, fat and fibre, it's protein that seems to be most in the news.
zh: 在碳水化合物、脂肪和纤维等主要食物类别中,蛋白质似乎是最常被提及的。

text: In shops and on social media, you'll find all kinds of high -protein foods being advertised, from shakes and yoghurts to chocolate.
zh: 在商店和社交媒体上,你会看到各种高蛋白食品的广告,从奶昔、酸奶到巧克力都有。

text: Here's Ruth Alexander, presenter of BBC World Service programme, the food chain.
zh: 下面请听BBC世界服务节目《食物链》主持人Ruth Alexander的报道。

text: Cast your eye over most aisles in the supermarkets now and you'll see high -protein products in abundance.
zh: 现在你只要扫一眼超市的大多数货架,就能看到大量高蛋白产品。

text: Pasta made with lentils and chickpeas rather than wheat flour.
zh: 用扁豆和鹰嘴豆而不是小麦粉做成的意大利面。

text: Bread fortified with beans and pulses. Ruth casts her eye over the supermarket shelves.
zh: 添加了豆类和豆科植物强化成分的面包。Ruth扫视着超市货架。

text: If you cast your eye over something, you take a quick look at it.
zh: 如果你cast your eye over(扫视)某样东西,意思是快速看一眼。

text: What she sees are protein products in abundance.
zh: 她看到的是大量的蛋白质产品。

text: In other words, in large quantities. products which already contain protein, like yoghurt, are repackaged as high protein,
zh: 换句话说,数量很多。像酸奶这类本身就含有蛋白质的产品,如今被重新包装成“高蛋白”产品,

text: while other items have extra protein added, often in the form of beans and pulses.
zh: 而其他一些产品则被额外添加了蛋白质,通常是以豆类和豆科植物的形式。

text: Protein is big business. Our bodies need protein for health and to build muscle.
zh: 蛋白质是一门大生意。我们的身体需要蛋白质来维持健康和增长肌肉。

text: But with all this focus on protein, are we forgetting about other important nutrients, such as fibre?
zh: 但在这种对蛋白质的高度关注下,我们是不是忽略了其他重要的营养素,比如纤维呢?

text: That's what we'll be discussing in this programme, hearing some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 这正是本期节目要讨论的内容,我们还会学到一些实用的新词和短语。

text: But First I have a question for you, Georgie. The popularity of protein started with bodybuilders
zh: 不过首先,我有个问题要问你,Georgie。蛋白质的流行最早始于健身爱好者,

text: and people working out in gyms who ate extra protein to gain muscle.
zh: 以及那些在健身房锻炼、为了增肌而摄入额外蛋白质的人。

text: Irishman Patrick Callaghan is a big name in the protein muscle world, but do you know who he is?
zh: 爱尔兰人Patrick Callaghan在蛋白质健身界可是个响当当的人物,你知道他是谁吗?

text: he a the owner of a company which makes protein shakes,
zh: a. 他是一家生产蛋白质奶昔公司的老板,

text: b the winner of the 2024 Mr Universe bodybuilding contest,
zh: b. 他是2024年“环球先生”健美比赛的冠军,

text: or c the scientist who discovered a new protein in outer space?
zh: c. 还是那个在外太空发现新型蛋白质的科学家?

text: Ooh, I'm going to say Patrick Callaghan is the winner of the 2024 Mr Universe bodybuilding contest.
zh: 哦,我猜Patrick Callaghan是2024年“环球先生”健美比赛的冠军。

text: OK, we'll find out if you're right at the end. Marketing campaigns that promote sales of high -protein food are certainly working.
zh: 好的,我们节目最后会揭晓答案对不对。促销高蛋白食品的营销活动确实很有效。

text: Sales of protein products make around £7 billion globally.
zh: 蛋白质产品的全球销售额约为70亿英镑。

text: Here's Scott Dicker from Spins, a market research company in Chicago, talking with BBC programme The Food Chain.
zh: 下面是来自芝加哥市场调研公司Spins的Scott Dicker,与BBC节目《食物链》的对话。

text: So sometimes carbs are good, sometimes they're bad for consumers.
zh: 有时候碳水对消费者是好的,有时候又被认为是不好的。

text: Sometimes fat is good, sometimes it's bad for consumers. But protein seems to have this continuous health halo on it.
zh: 有时候脂肪对消费者是好的,有时候又被认为是不好的。但蛋白质似乎一直笼罩着一层“健康光环”。

text: Unlike carbs, that's carbohydrates, and fat, consumers don't see any downside to eating protein.
zh: 不像碳水化合物和脂肪那样,消费者似乎看不到摄入蛋白质有任何负面影响。

text: Scott says protein has a health halo, a marketing term which describes perceiving a food to be be healthy based on a single feature,
zh: Scott所说的“健康光环”是一个营销术语,指的是仅凭某一个特征就认为某种食物是健康的,

text: such as being organic, gluten -free or in this case high protein, even if its overall nutritional value is poor.
zh: 比如有机、无麸质,或者像这里所说的高蛋白,即使它的整体营养价值其实很低。

text: Yes, food which used to be considered unhealthy is now called healthy just because a spoonful of protein powder has been added.
zh: 是的,一些原本被认为不健康的食品,如今只因为加了一勺蛋白粉,就被称作“健康食品”。

text: So could our modern obsession with protein mean that we ignore other important food groups like fibre?
zh: 那么,我们现代人对蛋白质的痴迷,是否意味着我们忽视了像纤维这样其他重要的食物类别呢?

text: NHS surgeon and blogger Dr Karen Rajan Jen thinks so
zh: 英国国家医疗服务体系(NHS)的外科医生兼博主Karen Rajan Jen医生认为确实如此,

text: and explains why here to Ruth Alexander on BBC World Service programme The Food Chain.
zh: 她向BBC世界服务节目《食物链》的Ruth Alexander解释了原因。

text: And do you think we're paying enough attention to fibre? Historically, probably not.
zh: 你觉得我们对纤维的重视程度够吗?从历史上看,可能不够。

text: I think the tide is turning and there's more people interested in fibre and optimising their fibre intake and fibre maxing even.
zh: 我觉得风向正在改变,越来越多的人开始关注纤维,优化自己的纤维摄入量,甚至出现了“纤维max”这种做法。

text: It's a trend on TikTok. What is fibre maxing?
zh: 这是TikTok上的一个潮流。什么是“纤维max”呢?

text: Fibre maxing is, you know, as it suggests, similar to the protein maxing trend
zh: “纤维max”顾名思义,和“蛋白质max”潮流类似,

text: where people are trying to maximise their fibre intake
zh: 就是人们想方设法最大化自己的纤维摄入量,

text: with little hacks or tricks or tips that they could do, whether it's adding certain seeds or nuts to snacks.
zh: 通过一些小窍门、小技巧或小贴士来实现,比如在零食里加些种子或坚果。

text: Many people pay more attention to protein than fibre in their diet,
zh: 很多人在饮食中更关注蛋白质,而不是纤维,

text: but Dr Karan thinks the tide is turning, an idiom which means that things are changing.
zh: 但Karen医生认为风向正在转变,这个习语的意思是“事情正在发生变化”。

text: In part, this is thanks to a new trend from America called fibre -maxing,
zh: 这在一定程度上要归功于源自美国的一种新潮流,叫做“纤维max”,

text: consuming lots of fibre by eating fibre -rich foods and supplements for their health benefits.
zh: 即通过食用富含纤维的食物和补充剂来大量摄入纤维,以获取健康益处。

text: Dr Karan also gives some hacks – good solutions or pieces of advice
zh: Karen医生还给出了一些“hacks”——也就是好办法或建议,

text: to help boost your fibre intake, for example adding seeds and nuts to
zh: 来帮助大家增加纤维摄入量,比如在餐食中加入种子和坚果。

text: meals. In fact, the healthiest diet is probably a balanced diet – one containing items from all the major food groups.
zh: 事实上,最健康的饮食大概就是均衡饮食——包含所有主要食物类别的饮食。

text: But I'm still intrigued by your question, Neil. Isn't it time you revealed the
zh: 不过Neil,我还是对你之前的问题很好奇。是不是该公布答案了?

text: answer? I asked you who the Irishman Patrick Callaghan was.
zh: 我问你,爱尔兰人Patrick Callaghan是谁,

text: And I said he was the winner of the Mr Universe contest.
zh: 我回答说他是“环球先生”比赛的冠军。

text: And you were correct. Yay! OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned in this episode.
zh: 你答对了!耶!好了,我们来回顾一下本期节目学到的词汇。

text: If you cast your eye over something, you have a quick look at it.
zh: 如果你cast your eye over某样东西,意思是快速看一眼。

text: If something is in abundance, there's a lot or more than enough of it.
zh: 如果某样东西in abundance,意思是数量很多,绰绰有余。

text: A health halo refers to overestimating how healthy an item is based on a single feature,
zh: health halo(健康光环)指的是仅凭某一个特征,就高估某样东西的健康程度,

text: such as being low in calories, gluten -free or high in protein.
zh: 比如低卡路里、无麸质或高蛋白。

text: The idiom the tide is turning means that things are changing.
zh: 习语the tide is turning意思是事情正在发生变化。

text: The trend fibre -maxing means people intentionally consume lots of fibre by eating fibre -rich foods and supplements
zh: “纤维max”这一潮流指的是人们有意识地通过食用富含纤维的食物和补充剂来大量摄入纤维,

text: for their health benefits. And finally, a hack is a good solution or piece of advice.
zh: 以获得健康益处。最后,hack指的是一个好办法或建议。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. But here's another hack for you.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。不过这里还有一个“hack”送给你。

text: Remember, you can find worksheets, quizzes and loads more resources to improve your English
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站上找到练习题、小测验以及更多帮助你提高英语水平的资源,

text: over on our website, site, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 网址是bbclearningenglish.com。

text: See you there soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们很快就在那里见,不过现在,先说再见啦。
